column1: What feature did Spring Boot 3.1 introduce to simplify the complexities of managing secure communications and trust materials?
An SSL Bundle encapsulates all trust material and configuration settings, such as keystores and private keys, into a single, easily manageable _____.
Under what primary prefix do configuration properties for SSL Bundles reside in `application.yaml` or `application.properties`?
To configure SSL Bundles that use Java Keystore (JKS) files, which configuration prefix is used?
To configure SSL Bundles using PEM-encoded text files, which configuration prefix is used?
What Java objects does Spring Boot automate the creation of based on SSL Bundle configurations, removing the need for manual management?
In the example of securing a `RestTemplate`, what method is called on the `RestTemplateBuilder` to apply a configured bundle?
Which bean can be injected into a Spring component to get programmatic access to all configured SSL Bundles?
To retrieve a specific named bundle from the `SslBundles` bean, what method is used?
What three Java interfaces form the structured abstraction layer introduced in Spring Boot 3.1 for SSL configurations?
Which Spring Boot 3.1 interface offers a gateway to `KeyStore` objects containing cryptographic keys and trusted certificates?
Which Spring Boot 3.1 interface coordinates and provides methods to manage `KeyManager` and `TrustManager` objects?
Which Spring Boot 3.1 interface serves as a one-stop shop, aggregating all SSL functionalities into a unified model?
To create a custom `SSLContext` from a retrieved `SslBundle` instance, what method should be called on the bundle?
For many data services, what property activates SSL support, leveraging the trust material in the Java runtime's `cacerts`?
For data services, which property allows you to apply a named SSL bundle to customize the trust material?
To configure SSL for a MongoDB connection in Spring Boot using an SSL Bundle, what are the three required properties under `spring.data.mongodb`?
Traditionally, what prefix was used for setting individual SSL configurations for an embedded web server in Spring Boot?
When using an SSL Bundle to secure an embedded web server, which single property is used to apply the bundle configuration?
What is a primary benefit of using SSL Bundles for embedded servers compared to the traditional `server.ssl.*` properties?
column2: Configurations are grouped, reusable, and less prone to mistakes.

column1: Besides `server.ssl`, what are two other configurations mentioned that also support the `.bundle` property for SSL setup?
Before Spring Boot 3.1 introduced SSL Bundles, developers often had to work directly with which classic Java class to store key material?
The SSL Bundle approach is particularly beneficial for reducing _____ risks and increasing the efficiency of managing SSL across multiple services.
A key advantage of SSL Bundles is that they lend themselves well to _____ management, allowing the same bundle to be reused across different parts of an application.
When configuring a JKS bundle in `application.yml`, which property under the keystore specifies the path to the keystore file?
When configuring a PEM bundle in `application.yml`, which property under the truststore specifies the path to the certificate file?
In a JKS bundle configuration, what property specifies the alias of the private key within the keystore?
The classic Java class `javax.net.ssl.SSLContext` acts as a factory for what two types of objects, orchestrating how SSL configurations are implemented at runtime?
